name: outlook-automation
description: Automate Outlook tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): emails, calendar, contacts, folders, attachments. Always search tools first for current schemas.
capabilities: Search and filter emails across all folders using KQL syntax
Query emails in specific folders with OData filters
List, create, and manage calendar events and schedules
List, create, and organize contacts and contact folders
Manage mail folders including create and list operations
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 73 static findings are false positives. The skill contains only documentation for Microsoft Outlook automation via Rube MCP (Composio). The flagged 'external_commands' are actually MCP tool names in documentation format (e.g., `RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS`, `OUTLOOK_SEARCH_MESSAGES`). The network URL is a legitimate MCP server endpoint. This is a safe, community-contributed skill for Outlook task automation.
valueStatement: Automate Microsoft Outlook operations including email search, calendar management, and contact organization through Claude Code and Rube MCP integration.
userTitle: Automate Outlook Email and Calendar Tasks
limitations: Requires Microsoft 365 or Enterprise accounts (not supported for @hotmail.com/@outlook.com)
Only works with Rube MCP (Composio) as the connection layer
Cross-folder search requires SEARCH_MESSAGES, not QUERY_EMAILS
Cannot filter emails by recipient or body content via OData

useCases.title: Email Management for Professionals
useCases.description: Search and organize work emails efficiently using natural language queries

useCases.title: Calendar Coordination
useCases.description: Check schedules and schedule meetings across calendars

useCases.title: Contact Organization
useCases.description: Create and organize contacts and contact folders for business directories

promptTemplates.title: Find Emails from Specific Sender
promptTemplates.prompt: Search my Outlook for emails from {sender_email} received in the last 30 days. List the subject, date, and preview of each message.
promptTemplates.scenario: Finding all emails from a specific contact

promptTemplates.title: List Unread High-Priority Emails
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me all unread high-importance emails in my inbox from this week.

promptTemplates.title: Check Calendar Availability
promptTemplates.prompt: What is my schedule for next Tuesday between 9 AM and 5 PM? Show free/busy status.

promptTemplates.title: Create Contact
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new contact named {name} with email {email} in my contacts.

bestPractices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get the current tool schemas before starting
Verify Outlook connection status is ACTIVE before running any workflows
Use the correct property names: email uses receivedDateTime, calendar uses start/dateTime
antiPatterns: Using email property fields (receivedDateTime) in calendar queries
Assuming @hotmail.com or @outlook.com accounts have full API access
Using display names instead of folder IDs for custom folders

faq.question: What accounts work with this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Microsoft 365 and Enterprise accounts. Personal accounts like @hotmail.com or @outlook.com have limited API access.

faq.question: Do I need API keys?
faq.answer: No API keys are needed. You only need to add the Rube MCP endpoint and complete Microsoft OAuth authentication.

faq.question: Can I search across all email folders?
faq.answer: Yes, use OUTLOOK_SEARCH_MESSAGES for cross-folder search. OUTLOOK_QUERY_EMAILS searches only a single folder.

faq.question: How do I check if Rube MCP is connected?
faq.answer: Call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS and verify it responds with available tools. Check connection status via RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS.

faq.question: What is KQL syntax?
faq.answer: KQL (Keyword Query Language) is used for email search. Examples: from:user@example.com, subject:invoice, hasattachment:yes

faq.question: Can I manage multiple calendars?
faq.answer: Yes, use OUTLOOK_LIST_CALENDARS to see available calendars, then specify calendar_id in other calendar operations.
